app.title: Bokuchi
app.noTabsOpen: No tabs open. Create a new tab to get started.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.basicUsageDescription: Bokuchi is a powerful Markdown editor with real-time preview. You can write Markdown content in the editor and see the rendered result instantly in the preview panel.
gettingStarted.newFileDescription: Create a new empty file to start writing.
gettingStarted.openFileDescription: Open an existing Markdown file from your computer.
gettingStarted.saveFileDescription: Save the current file to disk.
gettingStarted.splitViewDescription: Show editor and preview side by side for easy editing and reviewing.
gettingStarted.editorOnlyDescription: Focus on writing with only the editor visible.
gettingStarted.previewOnlyDescription: View the rendered result in full screen.

variables.title: Variables
variables.description: Variables allow you to use dynamic content in your Markdown files. You can define variables in the settings and reference them in your documents using {{variableName}} syntax.
variables.howToUseDescription: To use variables in your Markdown files, simply wrap the variable name in double curly braces. The variable will be replaced with its value when the document is rendered.
variables.settingUpDescription: Follow these steps to set up and use variables:
variables.step1Description: Click the menu button and select 'Settings' to open the settings dialog.
variables.step2Description: In the 'Global Variables' section, add variable names and their corresponding values.
variables.step3Description: Reference variables in your Markdown files using {{variableName}} syntax.

example1.title: Document Template
example1.description: Use variables for document metadata like title, date, and author.

example2.title: Company Template
example2.description: Use variables for company information like name, department, and version.

variables.tip1Description: Variable names cannot contain spaces. Use camelCase or snake_case for multi-word variables.
variables.tip2Description: Variable names are case-sensitive. Make sure to use the exact same case in your documents.
variables.tip3Description: Variables are global and can be used in all your documents. Changes to variables affect all open files.
variables.localVariablesDescription: You can also define variables within individual files using HTML comments. These local variables take precedence over global variables.
variables.localVariablesSyntaxDescription: Define local variables at the beginning of your Markdown file using the following syntax:
variables.localVariablesCodeExample:
<!-- @var title: My Document Title -->
<!-- @var author: John Doe -->
<!-- @var date: 2024-01-15 -->

# {{title}}

Author: {{author}}
Date: {{date}}
variables.priorityOrderDescription: When the same variable name exists in both local and global scope, the following priority order applies:
variables.localVsGlobalDescription: Use local variables for file-specific content and global variables for common content across multiple files.

features.title: Features
features.description: Bokuchi provides several powerful features to enhance your Markdown editing experience.

explorer.title: Explorer (Folder Tree)
explorer.description: Browse and open files directly from a folder tree sidebar. Open a folder to display its directory structure and quickly navigate between files.
explorer.persistentDescription: The explorer panel is always shown as part of the layout. When vertical tabs are enabled, it merges into a single sidebar with a resizable divider.
explorer.overlayDescription: The explorer panel slides in from the left as a temporary drawer.
explorer.offDescription: The explorer panel is hidden. Click the Explorer button in the header to open a folder and enable it.
explorer.highlight1: Lazy loading: Subdirectories are loaded on demand when expanded
explorer.highlight2: File filter: Show only Markdown files or all files
explorer.highlight3: Active file highlighting: The currently open file is highlighted in the tree
explorer.highlight4: Merged sidebar: Combines with vertical tabs in a single resizable panel

outline.title: Outline (Table of Contents)
outline.description: Navigate your document structure with an outline panel that displays all headings. Click any heading to jump to that section in the editor.
outline.persistentDescription: The outline panel is always shown on the right side of the editor.
outline.overlayDescription: The outline panel slides in from the right as a temporary drawer.
outline.highlight1: Automatic heading detection: Headings (H1-H6) are extracted in real-time as you type
outline.highlight2: Click-to-navigate: Click any heading to scroll the editor to that position
outline.highlight3: Indented hierarchy: Headings are displayed with indentation reflecting their level

tableConversion.title: Table Conversion Paste
tableConversion.description: Automatically convert HTML tables to Markdown format when pasting from spreadsheets, web pages, or other sources.
tableConversion.autoDescription: Tables are automatically converted to Markdown when pasted.
tableConversion.confirmDescription: A confirmation dialog with a preview is shown before converting the table.
tableConversion.offDescription: Tables are pasted as plain text without conversion.
tableConversion.highlight1: Detects HTML table data in the clipboard automatically
tableConversion.highlight2: Preview the converted Markdown table before applying
tableConversion.highlight3: Use Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+V to paste as plain text without conversion

keyboardShortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
keyboardShortcuts.description: Here are all the keyboard shortcuts available in Bokuchi:

tutorials.title: Quick Start Guide
tutorials.description: Get started with Bokuchi in just a few steps:

step1.title: Step 1: Create Your First Document
instructions: Press ⌘N (Mac) or Ctrl+N (Windows) to create a new file
Type your first heading: # My First Document
Add some content below the heading
Watch the preview update in real-time on the right

step2.title: Step 2: Save Your Work
instructions: Choose a location and filename for your document
Your file will appear in Recent Files for quick access

step3.title: Step 3: Use Variables for Dynamic Content
instructions: Add a variable like 'author' with your name
In your document, use {{author}} where you want your name to appear

step4.title: Step 4: Navigate Efficiently
instructions: Press ⌘R (Mac) or Ctrl+R (Windows) to open Recent Files
Use Ctrl+Tab to switch between open tabs
Use Ctrl+Shift+Tab to go to the previous tab

tips.title: Pro Tips
tips.tip1: Use F1 anytime to open this help system
tips.tip2: Try different themes in Settings > Appearance
tips.tip3: Use the split view to see your Markdown and preview side by side

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.themeDescription: Choose the visual theme for the application interface.

globalVariables.title: Global Variables
globalVariables.description: Global variables can be used in all files. Reference them in Markdown files as {{variableName}}.

language.title: Language
language.description: Select the language for the application interface.

tabLayout.title: Tab Layout
tabLayout.description: Choose how tabs are displayed in the editor.
tabLayout.horizontalDescription: Tabs are displayed horizontally above the editor.
tabLayout.verticalDescription: Tabs are displayed vertically on the left side of the editor.

viewMode.title: View Mode
viewMode.description: Choose how the editor and preview are displayed.
viewMode.splitDescription: Show editor and preview side by side.
viewMode.editorDescription: Show only the editor for focused writing.
viewMode.previewDescription: Show only the preview for reading.

editor.title: Editor
editor.fontSizeDescription: Adjust the font size for the editor.
editor.showLineNumbersDescription: Display line numbers in the editor.
editor.tabSizeDescription: Number of spaces for tab indentation.
editor.wordWrapDescription: Wrap long lines in the editor.
editor.minimapDescription: Show a minimap of the document.

interface.title: Interface
interface.zoomLevelDescription: Adjust the zoom level for the interface.
interface.outlineDisplayModeDescription: Choose how the outline panel is displayed.
interface.outlineDisplayModePersistentDescription: The outline panel is always shown as part of the layout.
interface.outlineDisplayModeOverlayDescription: The outline panel slides in from the right as an overlay.
interface.folderTreeDisplayModeDescription: Choose how the explorer (folder tree) panel is displayed.
interface.folderTreeDisplayModePersistentDescripti: The explorer panel is always shown as part of the layout.
interface.folderTreeDisplayModeOverlayDescription: The explorer panel slides in from the left as an overlay.
interface.folderTreeFileFilterDescription: Choose which files to display in the explorer.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.showWhitespaceDescription: Display whitespace characters in the editor.
advanced.tableConversionDescription: Convert HTML tables to Markdown when pasting.
advanced.renderingDescription: Enable or disable optional rendering features. Disabled features do not load their libraries, keeping the editor lightweight.
advanced.enableKatexDescription: Render math expressions using $...$ (inline) and $$...$$ (display) syntax.
advanced.enableMermaidDescription: Render diagrams from ```mermaid fenced code blocks (flowcharts, sequence diagrams, etc.).
advanced.resetConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset all settings to default? This action cannot be undone.
advanced.enableMarpDescription: Preview Markdown slides with marp: true front-matter as a presentation.

fileOperations.noMarkdownFiles: Please drop a Markdown file (.md or .markdown)
fileOperations.noActiveTab: No active tab to save. Please open a file first.

dialogs.confirmClose: Are you sure you want to close this tab?

fileChanged.title: File has been changed
fileChanged.question: Do you want to reload this file?
fileChanged.reloadDescription: • Yes: Load the latest file content (current edits will be lost)

easterEgg.lateNightMessage: Late night? Take a break once in a while.

tableConversion.dialogMessage: A table was detected in the clipboard. Would you like to convert it to Markdown format?
tableConversion.dialogNote: You can use Shift + Cmd/Ctrl + V to paste as plain text without conversion.
tableConversion.convertWithoutAsking: Convert without asking in the future
tableConversion.conversionFailed: Failed to convert table. Pasted as plain text.
tableConversion.noTableDetected: No table detected in clipboard data

katexMermaid.title: KaTeX & Mermaid Support
katexMermaid.description: The preview now renders LaTeX math expressions and Mermaid diagrams. Both can be toggled in Settings.

emptyState.title: New Welcome Screen
emptyState.description: A new welcome screen is displayed when no files are open, with quick access to create, open, or pick a recent file.

memoryOptimization.title: Memory Management Optimization
memoryOptimization.description: Closed tab caches are now released immediately, reducing memory usage.

bugFixes.title: Bug Fixes
bugFixes.description: Fixed file association issues and several other minor bugs.
